svg.title: Journey of a packet from your phone to the server
svg.desc: Shows a packet traveling from a mobile device through WiFi router, ISP, multiple internet routers, and arriving at the destination server, with packet structure shown below
svg.text: A packet's journey: your phone → Swiggy's server
Why this matters for system design
Every arrow in your architecture diagram = packets traversing this path.
More hops = more latency. Cross-region calls = 100-300ms RTT.
CDNs work by putting servers closer to the user = fewer hops.
Network partitions = a router in this chain fails = your services can't talk.
